modules/cache/mod_socache_dc.c

11 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14 * limitations under the License.
15 */
16
17#include "httpd.h"
18#include "http_log.h"
19#include "http_request.h"
20#include "http_config.h"
21#include "http_protocol.h"
22#include "mod_status.h"
23
24#include "apr_strings.h"
25#include "apr_time.h"
26
27#include "ap_socache.h"
28
29#include "distcache/dc_client.h"
30
31#if !defined(DISTCACHE_CLIENT_API) || (DISTCACHE_CLIENT_API < 0x0001)
32#error "You must compile with a more recent version of the distcache-base package"
33#endif
34
35struct ap_socache_instance_t {
36    /* Configured target server: */ view japanese
37    const char *target;
38    /* distcache client context: */ view japanese
39    DC_CTX *dc;
40};
41
42static const char *socache_dc_create(ap_socache_instance_t **context,
43                                     const char *arg,
44                                     apr_pool_t *tmp, apr_pool_t *p)
45{
46    struct ap_socache_instance_t *ctx;
47
48    ctx = *context = apr_palloc(p, sizeof *ctx);
49
50    ctx->target = apr_pstrdup(p, arg);
51
52    return NULL;
53}
54
55static apr_status_t socache_dc_init(ap_socache_instance_t *ctx,
56                                    const char *namespace,
57                                    const struct ap_socache_hints *hints,
58                                    server_rec *s, apr_pool_t *p)
59{
60#if 0
61    /* If a "persistent connection" mode of operation is preferred, you *must*
62     * also use the PIDCHECK flag to ensure fork()'d processes don't interlace
63     * comms on the same connection as each other. */ view japanese
64#define SESSION_CTX_FLAGS        SESSION_CTX_FLAG_PERSISTENT | \
65                                 SESSION_CTX_FLAG_PERSISTENT_PIDCHECK | \
66                                 SESSION_CTX_FLAG_PERSISTENT_RETRY | \
67                                 SESSION_CTX_FLAG_PERSISTENT_LATE
68#else
69    /* This mode of operation will open a temporary connection to the 'target'
70     * for each cache operation - this makes it safe against fork()
71     * automatically. This mode is preferred when running a local proxy (over
72     * unix domain sockets) because overhead is negligible and it reduces the
73     * performance/stability danger of file-descriptor bloatage. */ view japanese
74#define SESSION_CTX_FLAGS        0
75#endif
76    ctx->dc = DC_CTX_new(ctx->target, SESSION_CTX_FLAGS);
77    if (!ctx->dc) {
78        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, s, APLOGNO(00738) "distributed scache failed to obtain context");
79        return APR_EGENERAL;
80    }
81    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_INFO, 0, s, APLOGNO(00739) "distributed scache context initialised");
82
83    return APR_SUCCESS;
84}
85
86static void socache_dc_destroy(ap_socache_instance_t *ctx, server_rec *s)
87{
88    if (ctx && ctx->dc) {
89        DC_CTX_free(ctx->dc);
90        ctx->dc = NULL;
91    }
92}
93
94static apr_status_t socache_dc_store(ap_socache_instance_t *ctx, server_rec *s,
95                                     const unsigned char *id, unsigned int idlen,
96                                     apr_time_t expiry,
97                                     unsigned char *der, unsigned int der_len,
98                                     apr_pool_t *p)
99{
100    /* !@#$%^ - why do we deal with *absolute* time anyway???
101     * Uhm - because most things expire things at a specific time?
102     * Were the API were thought out expiry - r->request_time is a good approximation
103     */ view japanese
104    expiry -= apr_time_now();
105    /* Send the serialised session to the distributed cache context */ view japanese
106    if (!DC_CTX_add_session(ctx->dc, id, idlen, der, der_len,
107                            apr_time_msec(expiry))) {
108        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, s, APLOGNO(00740) "distributed scache 'store' failed");
109        return APR_EGENERAL;
110    }
111    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00741) "distributed scache 'store' successful");
112    return APR_SUCCESS;
113}
114
115static apr_status_t socache_dc_retrieve(ap_socache_instance_t *ctx, server_rec *s,
116                                        const unsigned char *id, unsigned int idlen,
117                                        unsigned char *dest, unsigned int *destlen,
118                                        apr_pool_t *p)
119{
120    unsigned int data_len;
121
122    /* Retrieve any corresponding session from the distributed cache context */ view japanese
123    if (!DC_CTX_get_session(ctx->dc, id, idlen, dest, *destlen, &data_len)) {
124        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00742) "distributed scache 'retrieve' MISS");
125        return APR_NOTFOUND;
126    }
127    if (data_len > *destlen) {
128        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, s, APLOGNO(00743) "distributed scache 'retrieve' OVERFLOW");
129        return APR_ENOSPC;
130    }
131    *destlen = data_len;
132    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00744) "distributed scache 'retrieve' HIT");
133    return APR_SUCCESS;
134}
135
136static apr_status_t socache_dc_remove(ap_socache_instance_t *ctx,
137                                      server_rec *s, const unsigned char *id,
138                                      unsigned int idlen, apr_pool_t *p)
139{
140    /* Remove any corresponding session from the distributed cache context */ view japanese
141    if (!DC_CTX_remove_session(ctx->dc, id, idlen)) {
142        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, s, APLOGNO(00745) "distributed scache 'remove' MISS");
143        return APR_NOTFOUND;
144    }
145    else {
146        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, s, APLOGNO(00746) "distributed scache 'remove' HIT");
147        return APR_SUCCESS;
148    }
149}
150
151static void socache_dc_status(ap_socache_instance_t *ctx, request_rec *r, int flags)
152{
153    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(00747)
154                  "distributed scache 'socache_dc_status'");
155    if (!(flags & AP_STATUS_SHORT)) {
156        ap_rprintf(r, "cache type: <b>DC (Distributed Cache)</b>, "
157                   " target: <b>%s</b><br>", ctx->target);
158    }
159    else {
160        ap_rputs("CacheType: DC\n", r);
161        ap_rvputs(r, "CacheTarget: ", ctx->target, "\n", NULL);
162    }
163}
164
165static apr_status_t socache_dc_iterate(ap_socache_instance_t *instance,
166                                       server_rec *s, void *userctx,
167                                       ap_socache_iterator_t *iterator,
168                                       apr_pool_t *pool)
169{
170    return APR_ENOTIMPL;
171}
172
173static const ap_socache_provider_t socache_dc = {
174    "distcache",
175    0,
176    socache_dc_create,
177    socache_dc_init,
178    socache_dc_destroy,
179    socache_dc_store,
180    socache_dc_retrieve,
181    socache_dc_remove,
182    socache_dc_status,
183    socache_dc_iterate
184};
185
186static void register_hooks(apr_pool_t *p)
187{
188    ap_register_provider(p, AP_SOCACHE_PROVIDER_GROUP, "dc",
189                         AP_SOCACHE_PROVIDER_VERSION,
190                         &socache_dc);
191}
192
193AP_DECLARE_MODULE(socache_dc) = {
194    STANDARD20_MODULE_STUFF,
195    NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL,
196    register_hooks
197};
198

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_socache_dc.c:36

/* 設定されたターゲットサーバー: */

Original comment

/* Configured target server: */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_socache_dc.c:38

/* distcache クライアントコンテキスト: */

Original comment

/* distcache client context: */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_socache_dc.c:61-63

/* 「持続的接続（persistent connection）」モードでの運用が好ましい場合は、fork() されたプロセス同士が同じ接続上で通信を混在させないよう、PIDCHECK フラグも必ず使用しなければなりません。 */

Original comment

/* If a "persistent connection" mode of operation is preferred, you *must*
     * also use the PIDCHECK flag to ensure fork()'d processes don't interlace
     * comms on the same connection as each other. */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_socache_dc.c:69-73

/* この動作モードでは、各キャッシュ操作のたびに 'target' への一時的な接続を開きます。これにより、fork() に対して自動的に安全となります。このモードは、ローカルプロキシ（Unixドメインソケット経由）を実行する場合に推奨されます。オーバーヘッドが無視できるほど小さく、ファイル記述子の肥大化によるパフォーマンスや安定性のリスクを低減できるためです。 */

Original comment

/* This mode of operation will open a temporary connection to the 'target'
     * for each cache operation - this makes it safe against fork()
     * automatically. This mode is preferred when running a local proxy (over
     * unix domain sockets) because overhead is negligible and it reduces the
     * performance/stability danger of file-descriptor bloatage. */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_socache_dc.c:100-103

/* !@#$%^ - そもそもなぜ*絶対*時間を扱う必要があるのか？？？
     * うーん - ほとんどのものは特定の時刻に期限切れになるからでは？
     * APIが期限切れを考慮して設計されていたなら、r->request_timeが良い近似値になる
     */

Original comment

/* !@#$%^ - why do we deal with *absolute* time anyway???
     * Uhm - because most things expire things at a specific time?
     * Were the API were thought out expiry - r->request_time is a good approximation
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_socache_dc.c:105

/* シリアライズされたセッションを分散キャッシュコンテキストに送信する */

Original comment

/* Send the serialised session to the distributed cache context */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_socache_dc.c:122

/* 分散キャッシュコンテキストから対応するセッションを取得する */

Original comment

/* Retrieve any corresponding session from the distributed cache context */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_socache_dc.c:140

/* 分散キャッシュコンテキストから対応するセッションを削除する */

Original comment

/* Remove any corresponding session from the distributed cache context */
